name: Turnip Greens and Cornmeal Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the ham hock and place it in an 8-quart Dutch oven. Add water and bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour or until the meat is tender.
3. Peel the turnip roots and cut them in half. Add the turnip greens, turnip roots, and 1 teaspoon of salt to the Dutch oven. Bring to a boil.
4. Cover and reduce heat. Simmer for 2 hours or until the greens and roots are tender.
5. In a bowl, combine cornmeal and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Mix well.
6. Stir in boiling water. Add the beaten egg and mix well.
7. Drop spoonfuls of the cornmeal mixture onto a well-floured board and roll them in flour.
8. Place the dumplings over the hot turnip greens. Cover and cook over medium heat for 15 minutes.
9. Serve immediately with turnip greens and pot likker.
This down-home dinner is a meal in itself. A Southern Heritage Vegetables Cookbook by Oxmoor House, Birmingham, Alabama.
